title: missing error wrapping in some podman pull code path
body:
### Issue Description

In see I saw a command error out with just `EOF` which is of course not helpful

```
# podman [options] --pull-option=enable_partial_images=true --pull-option=convert_images=true pull -q -a quay.io/libpod/testdigest_v2s2
Error: EOF
```

https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5462482640175104/html/int-podman-rawhide-root-host.log.html#t--Podman-pull-podman-pull-check-all-tags--1



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Don't know, without the error context it is hard to know which part failed an why.
I guess it would be best for someone to audit the code paths on pull to see where some error wrapping would be useful.

### Describe the results you received

`Error: EOF`

### Describe the results you expected

`Error: something something x: something y: EOF` with proper error context to know which operation really failed.

### podman info output

```yaml
podman@main
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Did some digging around this. Following the pull path, there are quite a few points where errors are returned unwrapped that bubble up to the user, `EOF` in your case. `GetRepositoryTags` is one such critical path. 

Given this is non-deterministic(networking hiccups), we should wrap bare err returns in the pull path and monitor future occurrences, if any. I'm not too sure about a widespread blanket hygiene cleanup, though.

We _could_ transfer this to c/common since this issue tracks the pull path specifically.

I mean adding a few wraps here and there is already useful if you found code paths were there is no wrapping at all 

IF the tag listing fails we need at least something like "getting repository tags: EOF" so at least we know what failed
Shall we move this to the container-libs repo? I've linked this issue for the time being in the PR.
